dfp_current.pdf.what: Bourbon County Schools District Facility Plan, KBE approval date August 2021 (current posted plan; next DFP due June 2025)

dfp_2021_excerpt.png.what: Wayback Machine capture of the same August 2021 plan, page 1 (school centers, enrollment/capacity, 2022-24 priorities)
dfp_2021_excerpt.png.source: web.archive.org capture of the education.ky.gov URL above (24 captures, Sep 2015 to Sep 2025)

dfp_2013_excerpt.png.what: Wayback Machine capture of the prior District Facilities Plan, KBE approval date June 2013 (school centers and post-2012-14 major renovation priorities)
dfp_2013_excerpt.png.source: web.archive.org capture of the education.ky.gov URL above

reading_note: Figures under 'Student Enrollment Capacity' read enrollment/capacity; confirmed by the 2021 preschool line 272/200 with the plan's own note that current enrollment is 80 full-day plus 192 half-day students (= 272).

dfp_2026_draft_excerpt.png.what: Bourbon County Schools District Facility Plan, 2026 planning-cycle draft, page 1, as presented at the July 15, 2026 Local Planning Committee forum, before the committee's amendment; KDE approval date and next-DFP-due both listed TBD. Annotated attendee copy (handwritten meeting notes visible). Shows KFICS capacities: BCHS 704, BCMS 544, Bourbon Central 640, Cane Ridge 547, North Middletown 154, Preschool 160; 2023-24 SAAR enrollments 783/560/491/461/128/209; six-year projections at minus 3 percent; North Middletown listed Permanent PS-5; new-construction priorities 1a and 1b both None; headline 2026-28 priority an $18,600,946 major renovation of the high school's 1968 and 1981 sections.
dfp_2026_draft_excerpt.png.source: photographed attendee copy from the July 15, 2026 forum

kfics_assessment.pdf.what: KFICS Facilities Assessment for Bourbon County Schools, prepared by RossTarrant Architects, presented at the July 2026 facility planning meeting (38 slides). Per-school needs (building condition plus instructional deficiency): BCHS $27,488,455; BCMS $22,418,692; Cane Ridge $14,387,595; Bourbon Central $8,840,267; North Middletown $8,530,093 ($5,648,434 condition + $2,881,659 instructional); Preschool $7,861,696; Agriculture Building $1,825,444. District total $98,441,294 ($91,352,242 school centers + $7,089,052 support). Slide capacities: BCHS 704, BCMS 544, Bourbon Central 499, Cane Ridge 397, North Middletown 154, Preschool 160 (note: the 2026 draft DFP table prints 640 and 547 for the two Paris elementaries, a discrepancy between the district's own documents).
kfics_assessment.pdf.source: presentation file shared from the district facility planning process
